introduction.p: Consider the line in this graph:

statement.p: Identify a point visible on this line that has integer coordinates, and write it as an ordered pair.

solution.p: The visible points with integer coordinates are (2,-1)\text{,} (3,2)\text{,} and (4,5)\text{.}

statement.p: What is the slope of the line?

solution.p: Several slope triangles are visible where the run is 1 and the rise is 3\text{.} So the slope is \frac{3}{1}=3\text{.}

statement.p: Use point-slope form to write an equation for this line, making use of a point with integer coordinates.

solution.p: Using (3,2)\text{,} the point-slope equation is y=3(x-3)+2\text{.} (You could use other points, like (2,-1)\text{,} and get a different-looking equation like y=3(x-2)+(-1) which simplifies to y=3(x-2)-1\text{.})
